'''Reece Lyne''' (born 2 December 1992) is an English [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|ce}} for [[Doncaster RLFC|Doncaster]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] and [[England national rugby league team|England]] at international level.

He has previously played for [[Wakefield Trinity]] and [[Hull F.C.]] in the [[Super League]], and played on the {{rlp|wg}} earlier in his career.

==Background==
Lyne was born in [[Kingston upon Hull]], [[Humberside]], England. He is of Jamaican descent.

==Early years==
He attended David Lister School from 2004 to 2009,<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/hull-fc-debut-remember-reece-lyne/story-11971013-detail/story.html|title=HULL FC: A début to remember for Reece Lyne|date=16 February 2010|work=Hull Daily Mail|accessdate=24 August 2016}}</ref> and also played for Ideal Isberg Rugby Club during his childhood years.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/isberg-strike-gold/story-11943755-detail/story.html|title=Isberg strike gold|date=16 December 2009|work=Hull Daily Mail|accessdate=24 August 2016}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Hull FC===
Lyne started his playing career in 2010 at [[Hull F.C.]]<ref>{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/hull/8790158.stm|title=Winger Reece Lyne signs new deal with Hull FC|date=5 July 2010|work=BBC Sport|publisher=BBC|access-date=2 October 2012}}</ref>

===Wakefield Trinity===
At the end of the 2012 season he signed a three-year deal with [[Wakefield Trinity]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/19787281|title=Reece Lyne moves to Wakefield Wildcats from Hull FC|date=1 October 2012|work=BBC Sport|publisher=BBC|access-date=2 October 2012}}</ref>
Having made his 100th appearance for the club at home to his old club [[Hull F.C.]], he wore the number four shirt for the 2017 season. Lyne played 15 games for Wakefield Trinity in the [[Super League XXVIII]] season as the club finished bottom of the table and were relegated to the [[RFL Championship]] which ended their 24-year stay in the top flight.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/20876/12962152/super-league-wakefield-trinity-relegated-after-loss-at-leigh-leopards-st-helens-and-wigan-warriors-win/|title=Super League: Wakefield Trinity relegated after loss at Leigh Leopards, St Helens and Wigan Warriors win|website=www.skysports.com|accessdate=29 September 2023}}</ref> During his 10 seasons with Wakefield he made 233 appearances and scored 64 tries.<ref name=DONoct23>{{cite web|url=https://www.doncasterrugbyleague.co.uk/article/1547/news-%7C-dons-sign-super-league-mainstay-reece-lyne |title= News {{!}} Dons sign Super League mainstay Reece Lyne|publisher=Doncaster RLFC |date=31 October 2023 |access-date=19 November 2023}}</ref><ref name=LRLoct23>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=31 October 2023|title=Wakefield Trinity stalwart Reece Lyne makes Championship switch on long-term contract: 'A really big signing for us'|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/wakefield-trinity-stalwart-reece-lyne-makes-championship-doncaster-switch-on-long-term-contract}}</ref>

===Doncaster RLFC===
On 31 October 2023, it was announced that Lyne had signed a three-year deal with [[Doncaster RLFC]].<ref name=DONoct23/><ref name=LRLoct23/>

==International career==
In July 2018 he was selected in the [[England Knights]] Performance squad.<ref name="Ten new additions in England Knights squad">{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/ten-new-additions-in-england-knights-squad/|title=Ten new additions in England Knights squad|publisher=loverugbyleague|date=24 July 2018|accessdate=31 October 2019}}</ref>

In 2018 he was selected for [[England national rugby league team|England]] against [[France national rugby league team|France]] at the [[Leigh Sports Village]].<ref name="Tom Johnstone marks debut with England hat-trick against France">{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/rugby-league/2018/10/17/tom-johnstone-marks-debut-england-hat-trick-against-france/|title=Tom Johnstone marks debut with England hat-trick against France|publisher=Guardian|date=17 October 2018|accessdate=31 October 2019}}</ref>

He was selected in [[England 9s]] squad for the [[2019 Rugby League World Cup 9s]].<ref name="Sam Tomkins eager to lift inaugural World 9s title with England Down Under">{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/sam-tomkins-eager-lift-inaugural-20595184|title=Sam Tomkins eager to lift inaugural World 9s title with England Down Under|publisher=Mirror|date=16 October 2019|accessdate=29 October 2019}}</ref>
